title: Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)
content:

Onasi]] *[[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Viddarie]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Visitors! Please depart! It's lost! It's lost!''"
"''Stop! What's going on here?''"
"''The ''Courageous''! The ''Courageous'' is lost!|A Telerath [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lucien Draay]], after the ''Courageous''{{'s}} destruction|}} The '''''Courageous''''' was an [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]] that served in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Produced by [[Rendili Hyperworks/Legends|Rendili Hyperworks]], the ''Courageous'' featured the ''Inexpugnable''-class' trademark "wishing-well" [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] and a massive [[Vanjervalis Systems]] [[Computer/Legends|computer]] fitted to its frame—the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was designed to coordinate the actions of dozens of vessels simultaneously. The ''Courageous'' served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] for [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] and participated in several campaigns on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. The ''Courageous'' saw action in the Republic's doomed [[Battle of Vanquo|defense]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vanquo]] in [[3964 BBY]]. The next [[3963 BBY|year]], the ''Courageous'' headed a [[Battle Group Serroco|battle group]] organized by Karath to hold the planet [[Serroco]] against a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] invasion. The ensuing [[Battle of Serroco]] resulted in the devastation of the planet and the shattering of the Republic defense. Mandalorian forces pursued the ''Courageous'' and its [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] through deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and eventually succeeded in boarding and destroying the vessel—Karath was barely able to escape with his life. Part of the ship was fashioned into a [[Mandalore the Ultimate's battle-axe|battle-axe]] wielded by the Mandalorian [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Mandalore the Ultimate]]. ==Description== {{Quote|If they're going to invade Serroco, they're going to need a lot more ships than they've brought!|Admiral Saul Karath, aboard the ''Courageous'' before the Battle of Serroco|}} [[File:Courageous.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Courageous'' in flight]] The ''Courageous'' was an [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]] manufactured by [[Rendili Hyperworks/Legends|Rendili Hyperworks]]. Per its design, the ''Courageous'' functioned as a platform from which the actions and movements of an entire [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] could be coordinated. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was distinguished by its "wishing-well" [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] configuration, which merged visual and electronic data by suspending [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] images over a transparent deck. The display could be disorienting to the crew, and vertigo was a common complaint.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' The bridge, circular in shape, had an open center surrounded by a number of [[Computer/Legends|computer]] stations. The ''Courageous'' featured a massive [[Vanjervalis Systems]] computer fitted to its frame, which, using the native software codes of a Republic [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]], could instantly transmit commands to as many as sixty-four Hammerheads simultaneously. As a result, the ''Courageous'' moved slowly, defended by the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] under its control. The ship had its own [[Comm unit/Legends|comm]] center, along with a [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] system capable of rapidly detecting the number and classification of enemy ships in its vicinity and the [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] signature of fired [[Missile/Legends|missiles]]. Measuring 3,100 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] wide, the ''Courageous'' had a sizable cargo hold that was connected to the ship's [[Prison/Legends|brig]] by way of ventilation tunnels. The ''Courageous'' itself was well-armed—the vessel featured five double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries and five light turbolaser batteries, along with a pair of [[Point-defense laser cannon/Legends|point-defense light laser cannon]] batteries and a pair of [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] batteries, all of which combined required sixty-six [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] to operate. The ''Courageous''{{'s}} [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] decks also held a sizable complement of 144 [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]], twenty-four [[S-250 Chela-class starfighter|S-250 ''Chela''-class starfighters]], twenty-four [[Conductor-class short-haul landing craft|''Conductor''-class short-haul landing craft]], six [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|''Ministry''-class orbital shuttles]], and other various support craft. The ''Courageous'' could hold as many as 50,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|tons]] of cargo and two [[Standard year/Legends|years]]' worth of [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]]. The ship had a Class 2 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and a Class 10 unit as a backup, and was equipped with a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]], [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], and [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. Although not available for sale, the ''Courageous''{{'s}} estimated value was 70 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. ==History== ===Early campaigns=== {{Quote|I'll deal with Carrick personally–provided we survive today!|Admiral Saul Karath, after encountering fugitive Zayne Carrick during the Battle of Vanquo|}} [[File:Battle_of_Vanquo.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The ''Courageous'' does battle at Vanquo.]] The ''Courageous'' was built at the [[Rendili/Vanjervalis Drive Yards]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], one of just six ''Inexpugnable''-class prototypes produced by the outbreak of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans. Designed to sell more Hammerhead [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], the ''Courageous'' was outfitted with a Vanjervalis computer system that allowed it to control up to sixty-four of the ships simultaneously. The ''Courageous'' was pressed into service when the Mandalorians invaded the Republic, and [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] was given to [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]], who had made his name hunting [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. In [[3964 BBY]], the Mandalorians began threatening vital resource worlds near the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and eventually moved on [[Vanquo]], a planet on the Republic's [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Karath and the ''Courageous'' saw action in the [[Battle of Vanquo]], the ship commanding the surrounding fleet from the center of the Republic line. In the midst of the [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]], Karath and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Dallan Morvis]] noticed a ship leaving Vanquo's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The vessel was identified as [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']], known to be carrying "[[Padawan Massacre|Jedi killer]]" and wanted Republic fugitive [[Zayne Carrick]], and it quickly jumped into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] toward Mandalorian territory. Karath took this to mean that Carrick was a Mandalorian [[Spy/Legends|spy]], and he vowed to bring Carrick to justice. The ''Courageous'' held out for as long as it could above Vanquo, but eventually Karath was forced to fall back and retreat. ===Serroco=== {{Dialogue|Morvis|Check that, check that. Bearing change on inbounds!|Karath|What?|Carrick|''No!!!''|Morvis|Missiles tracking around ''Courageous''! Repeat, around ''Courageous''! They weren't aimed at the fleet! They're heading for—|attr=Commander Dallan Morvis, Admiral Saul Karath, and Zayne Carrick, before the missiles impact Serroco|src=}} [[File:CourageousMissiles.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Nuclear bombs swerve around the ''Courageous'' during the Battle of Serroco.]] Karath's defense of Vanquo earned him a promotion to [[Rear Admiral/Legends|rear admiral]], and he soon turned his attention to the next front of the war. In [[3963 BBY]], the Mandalorians set their sights on the planet [[Serroco]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], a full member of the Republic,[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]] and the now-Admiral Karath formed what he called "[[Battle Group Serroco]]" in order to protect the world. Karath's battle group was centered around the ''Courageous'' and was bolstered by dozens of Hammerhead cruisers and other Republic ships, along with a fleet of [[Fringer/Legends|fringer]] [[Merchant/Legends|tradespeople]] who offered goods and services to the [[Republic soldier]]s. The ''Courageous'' and its group set off to Serroco from its staging point on [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]] ahead of the Mandalorians,[[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]] with Karath thinking that the Republic [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] was finally ready for its enemies. The ''Courageous'' took point above Serroco, surrounded by Hammerhead cruisers, while the rest of the battle group formed camps among the planet's [[Stereb]] [[Stereb cities|cities]]. Unbeknownst to Karath, Zayne Carrick was on the planet below, working at a [[Fringer/Legends|fringer]] [[Quartermaster-class supply carrier|mess ship]] called the ''[[Little Bivoli]]''. After the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] had a [[Force vision/Legends|Force vision]] of the Mandalorians destroying Serroco, Carrick decided that he needed to inform Karath of what he had seen, and he managed to convince Lieutenant [[Carth Onasi]], one of Karath's protégés, to give him a ride up to the ''Courageous''. Once Carrick stepped onto the ''Courageous''{{'s}} bridge, however, Karath, who already believed that he was a Mandalorian spy, immediately recognized Carrick and had him arrested. Karath refused to heed Carrick's warnings, and the Mandalorians soon arrived in-[[Serroco system/Legends|system]] and launched an opening barrage of [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear missiles]] toward the Republic fleet. To Karath's surprise, the bombs angled around the ships, aimed at the planetside camps all along. Twenty-seven populated areas were hit by the Mandalorian [[Battle of Serroco|attack]], and while Onasi saved many lives by using the ''Courageous''{{'s}} comm system to send the native Stereb to shelter in underground catacombs, the Stereb [[City/Legends|cities]] were wiped from the planet. ===Flight into oblivion=== {{Quote|Ah, Admiral Karath, is it? Or did you lose your rank as well as your flagship? Recognize this axe? It was forged from your so-called ''Courageous''–when we began melting it down for scrap!|Mandalore the Ultimate|[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]]}} [[File:BattleOfSerroco_dfna.jpg|thumb|right|225px|The ''Courageous''{{'s}} flight ends.]] With much of his Battle Group Serroco annihilated, Karath was left with no choice but to retreat and find a place to make a stand. The ''Courageous'' went on the run—Karath hoped to make it to the planet [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] or the [[Ryyk Nebula/Legends|Ryyk Nebula]], where the ''Courageous'' and its group could join forces with the [[Tremendous group|''Tremendous'' group]]. The Mandalorians pursued the ''Courageous'' every step of the way. As Karath had pledged to defend the ships in his [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] from Mandalorian attack, the ''Courageous'' saw plenty of action during its trip through the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]]. The Mandalorians eventually caught up to the ''Courageous'' in deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]], boarding the [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]] with their [[Neo-Crusader shock trooper|shock troopers]], the elite group thereby making its first appearance in the war. With his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] damaged and listing, Karath deemed that the only course of action was to scuttle the ''Courageous''. Karath ordered all nonessential personnel to the escape pods and prepared to make a stand against the boarders with his [[Executive officer/Legends|second-in-command]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Dallan Morvis; amidst the fighting, Karath narrowly escaped [[Death/Legends|death]] at the hands of a [[Shock trooper/Legends|shock trooper]] thanks to the timely arrival of Lieutenant Onasi. Trapped, Karath, Morvis, and Onasi made their way to the brig, sealing the door behind them, and checked with Zayne Carrick, who had been [[Meditation/Legends|meditating]] in his prison cell. Carrick rose to reveal that he had been spending his [[Time/Legends|time]] slowly [[Telekinesis/Legends|unfastening]] the bolts in the walls behind him with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], thus opening a passage from his cell to the cargo bay where Onasi's [[Conductor-class short-haul landing craft|cargo hauler]], the ''[[Deadweight]]'', sat unnoticed by the Mandalorians. With Onasi flying the ''Deadweight'', the small group of survivors were able to evade Mandalorian pursuit and narrowly escape the ''Courageous''{{'s}} destruction. Although news of the ''Courageous''{{'s}} fate filtered out and caused a panic on some Republic worlds, such as [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]], Karath managed to make his way back to the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he immediately submitted his resignation to the Republic Navy's [[Unidentified Admiral of the Fleet|Admiral of the Fleet]] for the loss of the ''Courageous''. Instead, Karath was placed in command of the battleship [[Swiftsure (Inexpugnable-class)|''Swiftsure'']], newly refitted based on knowledge that Karath had gained while serving on the ''Courageous''. The Mandalorians eventually melted down the ''Courageous'' for scrap, fashioning part of it into a [[Mandalore the Ultimate's battle-axe|battle-axe]] carried by their [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Mandalore the Ultimate]]. ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|It's your career or your life, Morvis. Don't take too long deciding.|Admiral Saul Karath, to his protégé Dallan Morvis, during the Mandalorian boarding of the ''Courageous''|}} [[File:Captainkarath.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Admiral Saul Karath, the ''Courageous''{{'s}} only commander]] The ''Courageous'' was commanded by Saul Karath, who was still waiting for the [[Merit Board]] to meet on his promotion to rear admiral when he was given control of the ship. The son of a [[Craddock Karath|laborer]] from Corellia, Karath made his name aboard the ship ''[[Reciprocity]]'', his first command, where he was noted for his efforts hunting pirates. Admiral Karath became renowned for his feats in the Mandalorian Wars, distinguishing himself in the heated defense of the planet [[Suurja]],{{KOTORbackups|0}} and then aboard the ''Courageous'' in the Vanquo and Serroco campaigns. Karath was presumed dead after the ''Courageous''{{'s}} destruction, and his safe return to Coruscant touched off joyful celebrations. One of Karath's men from the ''Reciprocity'', Commander Dallan Morvis, also served aboard the ship as Karath's right-hand man. Morvis came from an upper-class background—his [[Herron Morvis|father]] was the director of the [[Coruscant Financial Exchange]] and [[Morvis (senator)|mother]] the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] from [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]]—and he found a competitor for the admiral's favor in Lieutenant Carth Onasi, a hotshot [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] from [[Telos IV]] who shared Karath's hardscrabble beginnings. Both [[Gender/Legends|men]] rose in the ranks after the ''Courageous''{{'s}} destruction, with Morvis gaining command of his own [[Veltraa|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]][[Knights of the Old Republic 31|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 31]] and Onasi furthering his career in the Republic Navy under Karath.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' The ship's security chief was an individual named [[Viddarie]], and the ''Courageous'' also held up to 4,300 skilled crew and 2,000 [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Courageous'' first appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|eighth issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series. The issue was written by [[John Jackson Miller]], illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[September 20]], [[2006]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-894/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-8|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #8}} When designing the ship, Weaver took Miller's request for a "typical [[Warship/Legends|warship]] bridge with holographic battle displays" and created the ship's distinctive "wishing-well" design.{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/knight_errant_lego/index.html|text=Engineering with LEGOs: Creating the Ships of ''Knight Errant''}} Miller named the ship after the [[Wikipedia:French ship Courageux (1753)|''Courageux'']], a French warship captured by the British in 1761.{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor08.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #8''}} ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] {{1st}}
